Overview

Address
2026 EAST 54TH ST, INDIANAPOLIS, IN 46220
Phone
3172536950
Certified beds
150
Avg daily residents
127 (85% of beds filled)
Ownership
Government (county)
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1987-02-06
Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.45 4.23 3.15 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.61 0.74 0.55 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.57 0.94 0.52 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.27 2.55 2.07 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.17 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.08 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.89
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.46
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.63
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
49.2%
annual
RN turnover
40.0%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.50
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.09
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-02-25 9 8 1 40 1 40
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-01-10 24 17 12 120 1 120
Total weighted health score
60.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (52)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
51 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2025-07-01 2025-07-14
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-02-25 2025-03-21
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-02-25 2025-03-21
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-02-25 2025-03-21
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2025-02-25 2025-03-21
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2025-02-25 2025-03-21
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. D 2025-02-25 2025-03-21
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-02-25 2025-03-21
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-02-25 2025-03-21
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. C 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-29
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2023-08-29 2023-09-18
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2023-08-29 2023-09-18
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2023-06-20 2023-07-18
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-06-20 2023-07-18
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2023-03-28 2023-04-24
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2023-03-28 2023-04-24
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-03-28 2023-04-24
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2022-09-28 2022-11-10

Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
